description.html: Gas storage plays a critical role in providing a flexible and reliable energy system. Besides helping manage peak demand periods, it can be used for balancing out variability in supply from renewables. Many systems also employ the injection of hydrogen, a low-carbon fuel that has long-term potential for providing a bridge between current infrastructure and the future of cleaner, more efficient energy production.
